title: What Jack Kerouac Left Behind
description: More than half a century after “On the Road,” Jack Kerouac is still a literary celebrity. But fame undid the man I knew.
hook: Kerouac's fame destroyed the man she knew—a reckoning with how celebrity mythology erases the actual person beneath it.

title: Book Review: ‘The Danger to Be Sane,’ by Rosa Montero
description: In “The Danger to Be Sane,” the journalist Rosa Montero delves into the connections between psychic turbulence and creative work — including her own.
hook: Rosa Montero traces the line between psychological crisis and creative genius—her own included—with unflinching precision.

title: The Wrong Stalker
description: "He was locked up for a dangerous infatuation. Nothing was as it seemed."
hook: A man imprisoned for stalking, but nothing in this story is what it seems. An obsession that rewrites itself.

title: Come Shrine With Me: Holy Pop! at Somerset House
description:
A new exhibition at Somerset House explores fan-made shrines to dead pop stars, from Barnes' Marc Bolan memorial to Nina Simone's gum. Jak Hutchcraft talks to curator Tory Turk about art and fandom

Children of Graceland (project). Hayley Louisa Brown

How do we mourn a pop star? How do we remember somebody
hook: Fan-made shrines to dead pop stars reveal how grief transforms into ritual, devotion, and the need to be witnessed.

title: The Meanest Tradition in Entertainment
description: Celebrity roasts have become crueler in their style of comedy, and almost unwatchable.
hook: Celebrity roasts have become genuinely vicious—a shift that says something dark about what we now call entertainment.

title: 7 Books in Which Obsession Is the Plot
description:
All writing, at some level, grows out of obsession—the need to get our most intense and unwieldy feelings down on paper so that we might begin to see them clearly, or persuade others that our passions matter. But sometimes the obsession is right on the surface of the plot—is the plot. An obsession makes a […]

The post 7 Books in Which Obsession Is the Plot appeared first on
hook: Seven novels where obsession isn't subplot—it's the entire architecture, the thing that makes these books breathe.

title: A death doula’s advice on thinking about mortality
description: Death doulas, also called end-of-life doulas, wear many hats. In helping patients and their families prepare for a peaceful end of life, they can offer solace and companionship, handle logistics, mediate with medical staff, and more. As my colleague Anna North reported recently, public interest in the job is growing. Celebrities like actor Nicole Kidman […]
hook: Death doulas prepare families for the end of life. Their work is logistics, mediation, and profound companionship all at once.
